title: SDLC harness config
description: Project policy the SDLC engines read from planning/harness.json. The harness (engine code) carries the mechanism; this file carries the project facts (validation commands, whether a UI-test stage exists, ports/routes). If the file is absent, the engines fall back to the spec's `## Validation Commands` section and disable the UI-test stage — they ship no stack defaults. Shape: validation.checks[] (each a plain command or one of the richer kinds — baseline-diff / count-delta / warning-scan / forbidden-pattern-scan) + uiTest.enabled (and its enabled-only fields). The richer kinds keep all stack-specific commands and patterns here; the engine only carries the interpretation mechanism.

$schema.description: Editor hint pointing at this schema (e.g. ../.claude/workflows/harness.schema.json).

stack.description: Informational label only (e.g. 'rust', 'python-fastapi', 'nextjs'). Not consumed by engine logic.

_comment.description: Free-form note for humans editing the file. Ignored by the engines.

validation.description: The always-run validation suite the Test stage executes, in order.

checks.description: Ordered checks run top-to-bottom in the Test stage.

uiTest.description: Optional UI smoke-test stage. enabled:false makes the whole stage a no-op (verdict SKIPPED). The remaining fields are read only when enabled.

enabled.description: Whether the pipeline runs a UI smoke check. Non-web projects set false.

devServerCommand.description: Command that starts the dev server (e.g. 'npm run dev').

readySignal.description: Substring in the dev-server output that signals readiness (e.g. 'Ready in').

port.description: Base port the dev server listens on. In parallel task runs the engine uses port + taskNumber (hardcoded behavior).

routes.description: Routes to smoke-check once the server is ready (e.g. ['/', '/about']).

planning.description: Optional planning-phase policy read by the authoring commands (/plan, /feature, /generate-tasks). Absent → all fields default to preserve today's zero-touch behavior.

clarify.description: When true, the authoring commands surface 2-4 targeted clarifying questions for an ambiguous prompt BEFORE writing the spec. Default false preserves the zero-touch flow (write immediately). A user can always force this per-invocation with the --clarify flag regardless of this setting.

breakdown.description: Optional task-decomposition policy. Once the spec exists, the engines assess each task against a universal coarseness heuristic and, per `mode`, recommend or auto-generate a /breakdown before implementing. Absent → mode 'recommend' (advisory) with complexityThreshold 3. The heuristic is mechanism in the engines; only mode/threshold are project policy.

mode.description: recommend: log a breakdown recommendation for coarse tasks and proceed (no file written). auto: generate breakdown.md for the flagged tasks before implementing them (block: committed on main before the waves; standalone task: in its worktree). off: skip the assessment entirely.

complexityThreshold.description: A task that creates/modifies MORE than this many distinct files is a breakdown candidate (the file-count signal). Multi-concern / multi-layer tasks are flagged regardless of count.

block.description: Optional policy for the lean /sdlc-block runner (the 'more powerful sdlc-run': a fresh implement agent per task, shared setup once, one consolidated back-half). Absent → verify 'consolidated' (per-task review off). Only read by /sdlc-block; /sdlc-run and /sdlc-task ignore it.

verify.description: How much per-task verification the lean block does before its single consolidated back-half (test → review → fix → document → wrap-up over the integrated tree). 'consolidated' (default, cheapest): each task runs implement + the D8 completeness self-check only; localization happens once at the end. 'consolidated+review': each task additionally gets one review pass (a localization map for the consolidated fix) — recommended by /generate-tasks when tasks are large/heterogeneous enough that end-only localization is hard (≈38k tokens × N). The consolidated review stays authoritative in both modes; per-task review never substitutes for it. CLI override: --verify-depth <value>.

check.description: One validation check. With no `kind` (or kind:'command') it is a plain command gated on exit code — the original shape. The richer kinds add engine-interpreted semantics while keeping every stack-specific command/pattern in this file.

kind.description: How the engine runs this check. Omit (or 'command') for a plain exit-code gate. 'baseline-diff': snapshot a baseline at worktree creation and fail only on net-new items. 'count-delta': extract an integer and fail when it regresses vs the previous task. 'warning-scan': run a command (its exit code gates) and record matches of warningPatterns in its output, with severity per `gates`. 'forbidden-pattern-scan': grep source for patterns that must not appear.

command.description: Shell command to run. Required for all kinds except forbidden-pattern-scan. For baseline-diff this produces the CURRENT snapshot (same format as baselineCommand). For count-delta / warning-scan it is the command whose output is parsed / scanned.

purpose.description: One-line description of what the check gates. For the authoritative test command, say so here.

gates.description: Whether this check's failure blocks the review verdict. Replaces the old hardcoded 'jest is authoritative' rule. For warning-scan, gates:false reports matches as WARN; gates:true fails on any match.

baselineCommand.description: baseline-diff only: run once at worktree creation to capture the pre-task baseline artifact. Must emit the same format as `command` (e.g. a JSON array of violation objects).

compareKeys.description: baseline-diff only: the object fields whose tuple identifies a result item. The check fails on items in the current output whose key-tuple is absent from the baseline (net-new only); pre-existing items are ignored.

countPattern.description: count-delta only: a grep -E (ERE) pattern matching the line that carries the count; the engine extracts the first integer on the matched line (e.g. '[0-9]+ tests? collected').

failOn.description: count-delta only: fail when the count drops below the previous task's recorded count ('decrease'), or also when it fails to increase ('zero-or-decrease'). First task / no prior record → the check is SKIPPED.

warningPatterns.description: warning-scan only: grep -E (ERE) patterns matched against the command's combined stdout+stderr. Every match is recorded in the report; gating follows `gates`.

rules.description: forbidden-pattern-scan only: source patterns that must NOT appear. Any rule that matches is a violation.

rule.description: A forbidden-pattern-scan rule: source matching `pattern` (minus `allowlistPattern`) is a violation.

pattern.description: A grep -E (ERE) pattern searched across the project's source.

paths.description: Optional path scope for the grep (e.g. 'app/'). Defaults to the whole worktree.

allowlistPattern.description: Optional grep -E pattern; matching lines are excluded as legitimate exceptions.
